incident_title: Cascading Checkout Failure — Auth Service Timeout Triggering Downstream Collapse
goal: Write a complete incident post-mortem for a cascading failure across 3 services. Identify which service was the ROOT cause (not just the first to show symptoms). Use QUERY_LOGS carefully — the key evidence is in a non-obvious window. Assign action items to the correct teams.

initial_alerts.message: checkout error rate at 12% — threshold is 5%
auth token validation p99 at 4.8s — timeout threshold 5s
order-service failing to validate sessions — auth returning 504
Multiple upstream services DOWN: checkout, auth
PagerDuty: SEV-1 fired — multiple services down
Auth DB read replica promoted to primary
checkout health check PASSED — cascading recovery complete

slack_thread.author: pagerduty-bot
slack_thread.text: 🚨 SEV-1: checkout AND auth are DOWN. On-call: @priya @tom

slack_thread.author: priya
slack_thread.text: checkout is erroring on every request. Looking at it now.

slack_thread.author: priya
slack_thread.text: checkout logs show auth is timing out on every token validation call. Checkout itself seems fine — it's blocked on auth.

slack_thread.author: alex
slack_thread.text: Heads up — we deployed a new Redis caching layer for auth at 13:55. Could be related.

slack_thread.author: tom
slack_thread.text: Checking auth. Redis cache looks healthy. Memory usage normal.

slack_thread.author: tom
slack_thread.text: Wait — auth is hammering its DB. Read replica is lagging 45 seconds behind primary. That's the bottleneck.

slack_thread.author: priya
slack_thread.text: So auth DB replica is the root cause? Not Redis?

slack_thread.author: tom
slack_thread.text: Correct. Redis was a red herring — it's healthy. Auth's DB read replica is overwhelmed. Promoting replica to primary now.

slack_thread.author: alex
slack_thread.text: Could the Redis deployment have caused extra load on the auth DB? If Redis cache misses are high, auth would fall back to DB reads.

slack_thread.author: tom
slack_thread.text: Good catch Alex. Checking cache hit rate... yes. Cache hit rate dropped to 8% after the Redis deploy. Auth is doing 10x normal DB reads.

slack_thread.author: tom
slack_thread.text: Root cause confirmed: Redis deployment misconfigured TTL (set to 1s instead of 300s). Causes cache misses → auth DB overwhelmed → auth timeouts → checkout blocked.

slack_thread.author: tom
slack_thread.text: Promoting auth DB read replica to primary as interim fix. Will fix Redis TTL config properly.

slack_thread.author: priya
slack_thread.text: All services recovering. Checkout green, order-service green.

slack_thread.author: tom
slack_thread.text: Post-mortem needed. Real root cause: Redis TTL misconfiguration in the 13:55 deploy.

logs.message: Redis cache deployment started — new config applied
Cache miss — fetching token from postgres-auth [cache_hit_rate: 91%]
Cache miss — fetching token from postgres-auth [cache_hit_rate: 54%]
Cache miss — fetching token from postgres-auth [cache_hit_rate: 8%]
Read replica lag: 45s — queries being rerouted to primary
Replica health check FAILED — too far behind primary

valid_phrasings: Redis TTL misconfigured to 1s instead of 300s
Redis cache TTL set incorrectly causing cache misses
Redis deployment set TTL to 1 second causing auth DB overload
Cache misconfiguration in redis-auth leading to DB saturation

red_herrings: Redis cache memory usage was normal — memory is not the issue
Checkout service code was not changed — it is a victim, not a cause
The Redis deployment mention comes BEFORE checkout starts failing — timeline matters
